title: Dysphagia

presenting_history.text: Supports adult recurrent epigastric pain, heartburn, acid regurgitation, bloating, nausea or vomiting, associated precipitants, meal timing, and clinician-discussed lifestyle documentation.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports mapped adult reflux-symptom context only.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports adult recurrent epigastric pain, heartburn, acid regurgitation, bloating, nausea or vomiting, associated precipitants, meal timing, and clinician-discussed lifestyle documentation.

source.title: Gastro-oesophageal reflux disease and dyspepsia in adults: investigation and management

exact_section.heading: Scope and recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.2 and 1.4.1 — symptom profile and common care

red_flags.text: Supports immediate specialist referral for significant acute gastrointestinal bleeding, medicine-cause review, prior endoscopy review, and further assessment when new alarm signs are present.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped previous-endoscopy review and alarm-context documentation without automatically ordering endoscopy.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports immediate specialist referral for significant acute gastrointestinal bleeding, medicine-cause review, prior endoscopy review, and further assessment when new alarm signs are present.

source.title: Gastro-oesophageal reflux disease and dyspepsia in adults: investigation and management

referral.text: Supports clinician recognition of dysphagia, weight-loss combinations, and jaundice in recommendation-specific age and symptom contexts; it does not assert cancer or an automatic referral decision.
referral.rationale: Supports mapped dysphagia presentation, weight-loss context, and clinician-decided referral documentation without asserting cancer.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician recognition of dysphagia, weight-loss combinations, and jaundice in recommendation-specific age and symptom contexts; it does not assert cancer or an automatic referral decision.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.7 — oesophageal, stomach, and pancreatic cancer features

follow_up.text: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.
follow_up.rationale: Supports mapped warning-symptom, return, and agreed-review documentation without fixed timing.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.

source_limitations: The exact sources do not cover all ages or all oropharyngeal, neurological, structural, and specialist-swallowing contexts.

Duration, solids-versus-liquids distinction, progression, odynophagia, haematemesis negative, oral or throat examination, vitals, BMI, hoarseness or cervical nodes, family history, regurgitation, cough or choking, blood tests, generic previous records, barium swallow, fixed follow-up intervals, PRN wording, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
Cause, diagnosis, aspiration risk, urgency, investigation choice, endoscopy decision, treatment, and referral route remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
